rulings.Case Title: NANCY MGGINNIS VS. SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 3, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANT SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING INC., ABS LOAN TRUST VI DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint is continued to October 20, 2020. Parties are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of their respective filings (opposition and reply) by no later than September 21, 2020 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The Court received courtesy copies of the moving papers. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RACHEL D. WAGNER ET AL VS. CAM H. LUU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 3, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT CAM LUU, ANTHONY LUU, CAM PHAM, CHRISTINE LUU DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED as follows: This action is abated until Probate Action is resolved. Upon the final resolution of the Probate Action either party may file appropriate notification of such resolution and proceed with this action, if necessary and proper at that time. Parties requests to consolidate and transfer made in opposition and reply are denied as procedurally improper. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NASSER S. JALALI ET AL VS. FIVE STAR INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 3, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF NASSER JALALI, ALEXANDER JALALI Notice Of Motion Plaintiff's Motion to Strike Demurrer. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Strike Demurrer is DENIED. Response to the Complaint was filed prior to any default being properly taken. No authority is cited for the proposition that the demurrer should be stricken under such circumstances. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NASSER S. JALALI ET AL VS. FIVE STAR INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 3, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT FIVE STAR INVESTMENTS, LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person (a good faith reason for personal appearance is required) for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Demurrer cannot be sustained only to a part of a cause of action. All causes of action are in part based on allegations of use that are not addressed by the demurrer specifically (e.g. argument regarding illegality) and are sufficient to support all stated causes of action (e.g. paragraphs 5(a), (c), (d), 11, etc. Declarations filed by the parties are not considered on this demurrer. Defendants Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. =(501/CFH)
